content.problemStatement:
An array BBB of length MMM consisting of only **distinct** elements is said to be good if the following condition holds:


Let iii be the index of the maximum element of BBB.
Then, B[1,i]B[1,i]B[1,i] must be in ascending order, i.e, B1<B2<B3<…<BiB_1 \lt B_2 \lt B_3 \lt \ldots \lt B_iB1​<B2​<B3​<…<Bi​.
For example, [1,2,3],[1,4,3,2][1, 2, 3], [1, 4, 3, 2][1,2,3],[1,4,3,2] and [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] are good arrays, while [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3] and [3,2,4,1][3, 2, 4, 1][3,2,4,1] are not.

You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN. You have to partition PPP into some non-empty **good** subarrays. Note that every element of PPP should be contained in **exactly** one subarray.

Find the total number of valid partitions.

Since the answer can be quite large, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

**Note:** A permutation of length NNN is an arrangement of the integers {1,2,3,…,N}\{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N\}{1,2,3,…,N}.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated distinct integers P1,P2,P3,…,PNP_1, P_2, P_3 , \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,P3​,…,PN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid partitions modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: 1≤Pi≤N1 \leq P_i \leq N1≤Pi​≤N for each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
